title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 41 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: New Year's Day
festivals.description: Finland's primary national public holiday marking the start of the new year with celebrations across the country.

festivals.name: Laskiainen (Shrove Tuesday)
festivals.description: A traditional carnival celebration where Finns enjoy sledding and eating sweet pastries called laskiaispulla.

festivals.name: Ruisrock
festivals.description: One of the oldest and largest rock festivals in the world, held on Ruissalo island with diverse international and local artists.

festivals.name: Good Friday
festivals.description: A Christian public holiday in Finland observed with religious services and traditional customs.

festivals.name: Easter Sunday
festivals.description: A major Christian holiday celebrated with family gatherings, traditional foods, and Easter egg hunts.

festivals.name: Easter Monday
festivals.description: A public holiday marking the second day of Easter, giving Finns an extended celebration period.

festivals.name: Vappu (May Day)
festivals.description: Finland's most vibrant street celebration marking spring with parades, outdoor parties, and communal gatherings.

festivals.name: Savonlinna Opera Festival
festivals.description: A prestigious international opera festival held in a medieval castle setting, attracting world-class performers and opera enthusiasts.

festivals.name: Ascension Day
festivals.description: A Christian public holiday celebrated 40 days after Easter with religious observances and family activities.

festivals.name: Whit Sunday
festivals.description: A Christian holiday marking the descent of the Holy Spirit, observed with special church services and festive traditions.

festivals.name: Midnight Sun Film Festival
festivals.description: A unique film festival held during the midnight sun period showcasing cinema in an open-air setting under continuous daylight.

festivals.name: Midsummer (Juhannus)
festivals.description: Finland's most important celebration honoring the summer solstice with bonfires, saunas, and traditional customs in cottages and countryside.

festivals.name: Turku Music Festival
festivals.description: A major classical music festival featuring renowned international orchestras and soloists performing across Turku's historic venues.

festivals.name: Provinssirock
festivals.description: One of Finland's largest rock festivals featuring international and Finnish artists in a scenic outdoor setting.

festivals.name: Kuhmo Chamber Music Festival
festivals.description: A world-renowned chamber music festival attracting elite musicians and classical music aficionados to the Finnish countryside.

festivals.name: Sonisphere Festival
festivals.description: A heavy metal and rock festival featuring international bands and a vibrant festival atmosphere in Tampere.

festivals.name: Floora Festival
festivals.description: A summer festival celebrating music, culture, and local food in a beautiful coastal setting.

festivals.name: Ilosaarirock
festivals.description: A major rock festival with international headliners held on an island in eastern Finland.

festivals.name: Tuska Metal Festival
festivals.description: Europe's largest heavy metal festival featuring global metal acts in Helsinki's beautiful park setting.

festivals.name: Kalevala Night
festivals.description: A celebration honoring the Finnish national epic Kalevala with cultural performances and traditional activities.

festivals.name: Turku Medieval Fair
festivals.description: A popular event recreating medieval life with craftspeople, performers, and historical demonstrations in Turku Castle.

festivals.name: Independence Day
festivals.description: Finland's national independence day celebrating the country's independence with traditional ceremonies and festivities.

festivals.name: Christmas Eve
festivals.description: A public holiday marking Christmas with family gatherings, sauna visits, and traditional Finnish Christmas traditions.

festivals.name: Christmas Day
festivals.description: A major Christian public holiday celebrated with family meals and church services across Finland.

festivals.name: Boxing Day
festivals.description: A public holiday following Christmas where many Finns continue festive celebrations and outdoor activities.

festivals.name: Lahti International Winter Sports Festival
festivals.description: A historic winter sports competition featuring ski jumping, cross-country skiing, and biathlon in iconic Finnish snow sports tradition.

festivals.name: Tampere Biennale
festivals.description: An international contemporary music festival showcasing cutting-edge classical and avant-garde performances.

festivals.name: Savonlinna Castle Festival
festivals.description: A cultural festival with theater, music, and exhibitions held within the atmospheric medieval Savonlinna Castle.

festivals.name: Arctic Circle Music Festival
festivals.description: A summer music festival celebrating diverse genres in the Arctic region with unique midnight sun performances.

festivals.name: Lakeus Jazz Festival
festivals.description: A jazz music festival featuring international and Finnish jazz artists in a relaxed summer setting.

festivals.name: Pori Jazz
festivals.description: One of Europe's most important jazz festivals attracting world-class musicians to the coastal town of Pori.

festivals.name: Naantali Music Festival
festivals.description: A classical music festival set in the picturesque medieval town of Naantali with intimate performances.

festivals.name: Finland's Independence Centennial Celebration
festivals.description: Annual celebration of Finland's independence with national events, ceremonies, and patriotic festivities.

festivals.name: Imatra Deep Sound Festival
festivals.description: An electronic music festival with international DJs set against the backdrop of the dramatic Imatra rapids.

festivals.name: Arctic Winter Games
festivals.description: An international sporting event featuring traditional Arctic sports and winter activities with competitors from circumpolar regions.

festivals.name: Jyväskylä Summer Festival
festivals.description: A major cultural festival showcasing music, theater, dance, and visual arts in central Finland's largest city.

festivals.name: Oulu Music Video Festival
festivals.description: A unique festival dedicated to music video art featuring international and Finnish artists' creative visual works.

festivals.name: Aland Islands Midsummer
festivals.description: A distinctive island celebration of midsummer with traditional customs, maypole dancing, and coastal festivities.

festivals.name: Hämeenlinna Summer Opera
festivals.description: A classical opera festival set in the historic Hämeenlinna Castle featuring performances in an atmospheric medieval setting.

festivals.name: Lappeenranta Fortress Summer Theater
festivals.description: Theatrical performances held within the historic Lappeenranta Fortress during the summer season.

festivals.name: Mikkeli Winter Festival
festivals.description: A winter celebration featuring ice sports, traditional Finnish winter activities, and festive entertainment.
